title: PowerShell Report Generation - ImportExcel, PSWriteHTML, CSV Export, And Power BI Refresh
applies_to: Generating formatted Excel reports from Qualys, BitSight, or Defender data in PowerShell
Creating HTML reports with interactive tables for email or web distribution
Triggering Power BI dataset refresh after data pipeline completion
Exporting structured CSV data for downstream consumption

confirmed_facts.fact: The `ImportExcel` module (by Doug Finke) creates and modifies Excel .xlsx files without Excel installed. Install: `Install-Module ImportExcel -Scope CurrentUser`. Core export cmdlet: `$data | Export-Excel -Path $xlsxPath -WorksheetName 'Sheet1' -AutoSize -FreezeTopRow -BoldTopRow -TableName 'DataTable' -ClearSheet`. The `-ClearSheet` flag overwrites existing worksheet content on repeated runs.

Multiple worksheets in one workbook using ImportExcel: call `Export-Excel` multiple times with different `-WorksheetName` values and add `-Append` on the second and subsequent calls to write to the same file without overwriting earlier sheets. Example: `$qualysData | Export-Excel -Path $path -WorksheetName 'Qualys' -AutoSize; $bitsightData | Export-Excel -Path $path -WorksheetName 'BitSight' -Append -AutoSize`.

Conditional formatting with ImportExcel: `$ws = (Open-ExcelPackage -Path $path).Sheet1; Add-ConditionalFormatting -WorkSheet $ws -Address 'D2:D500' -RuleType LessThan -ConditionValue 700 -ForeGroundColor Red -BackgroundColor LightPink`. Useful for highlighting BitSight ratings below threshold or Qualys critical counts above zero.

Chart generation with ImportExcel: `Add-ExcelChart -WorkSheet $ws -ChartType ColumnClustered -XRange 'A2:A20' -YRange 'D2:D20' -Title 'Security Ratings' -Column 7 -Row 1`. Charts are embedded in the worksheet and visible when the file is opened in Excel.

CSV export: `$data | Export-Csv -Path $csvPath -NoTypeInformation -Encoding UTF8`. The `-NoTypeInformation` flag removes the `#TYPE` header comment from the first line. `-Encoding UTF8` is required to correctly handle special characters; omitting it uses the system default encoding which may corrupt non-ASCII content.

HTML report generation with `PSWriteHTML` module (by Przemyslaw Klys): `Install-Module PSWriteHTML -Scope CurrentUser`. Create self-contained interactive HTML: `New-HTML -FilePath $htmlPath -TitleText 'Security Report' { New-HTMLSection -HeaderText 'BitSight Ratings' { New-HTMLTable -DataTable $bitsightData -HideFooter -SearchBuilder -PagingOptions @(25,50,100) } }`. Produces a self-contained HTML file with embedded DataTables.js.

Send report as email attachment via Microsoft Graph (no SMTP relay required): acquire a token for scope `https://graph.microsoft.com/.default` with Mail.Send permission, then call `Send-MgUserMail -UserId $senderUpn`. Attach files by converting to Base64: `[Convert]::ToBase64String([IO.File]::ReadAllBytes($filePath))` and including in the message body's `attachments` array.

Trigger a Power BI dataset refresh via REST API from PowerShell: acquire a Power BI service token with scope `https://analysis.windows.net/powerbi/api/.default`, then: `Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://api.powerbi.com/v1.0/myorg/datasets/$datasetId/refreshes" -Method POST -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $pbiToken" }`. A 202 Accepted response means the refresh was queued.

Check Power BI refresh status: `Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://api.powerbi.com/v1.0/myorg/datasets/$datasetId/refreshes" -Method GET -Headers @{ Authorization = "Bearer $pbiToken" }`. Returns a `value` array of refresh history entries. Most recent entry status: `Completed`, `Failed`, or `Unknown` (in progress).

implementation_notes: For security reporting dashboards, use ImportExcel with separate worksheets per data source (Qualys, BitSight, Defender) plus an Executive Summary sheet with KPI values. This lets business users navigate per-source data while seeing top-line metrics on one sheet.

Apply conditional formatting after the main Export-Excel call using Open-ExcelPackage / Add-ConditionalFormatting / Close-ExcelPackage. This keeps the main export clean and makes formatting changes easy to update independently.

For Power BI integration, the simplest pattern is to upload the CSV or Excel file to a SharePoint document library and configure Power BI to connect to that file as a data source with scheduled refresh. Avoid the REST refresh trigger unless you need real-time refresh coordination.

PSWriteHTML is best for email distribution or intranet publishing where recipients may not have Excel. Use it for weekly summary reports; use ImportExcel for analyst-facing workbooks that will be manipulated after delivery.

When emailing via Graph, note that Mail.Send is a broad permission. For automation, use a dedicated service mailbox rather than a real user's mailbox to contain the permission scope and preserve audit trails.
caveats: ImportExcel requires the EPPlus library and does not support all Excel features (e.g., complex macros, pivot table refresh). It is sufficient for formatted tables, charts, and conditional formatting.
The PSWriteHTML `-ShowHTML` parameter opens the file in the default browser. Omit it in automated scripts.
Power BI dataset refresh via REST is subject to the plan's refresh-per-day limit (8/day for Pro, 48/day for Premium). Exceeding this returns 429.

Export-Csv in PowerShell 5.1 with `-Encoding UTF8` writes a UTF-8 file with BOM. In PowerShell 7+, use `-Encoding utf8NoBOM` to produce a BOM-free file, which is better for cross-platform consumption.
The Power BI REST token uses a different resource scope than Microsoft Graph. Acquire it separately from your Graph token even if using the same app registration.
